target.title: Doctor: structure sandbox repairs
target.body:
## Summary

- Replaces the old broad sandbox PR with a focused doctor slice stacked on #84290.
- Extracts sandbox registry, image, and scope detection helpers from the legacy sandbox doctor flow.
- Registers internal core health checks for legacy sandbox registry files, sandbox image readiness, and shared-scope sandbox warnings.
- Runs sandbox registry/image repairs positionally at the existing sandbox contribution, while preserving legacy non-fix note presentation and scope warnings.
- Keeps plugin, policy, gateway service, session, SDK, and public health contract surfaces untouched.

## Verification

- git diff --check
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot: 4 files / 62 tests passed after rebase onto #84290
- codex review --base fork/doctor-next-plugin-registry: no actionable regressions
- previous full slice proof on this branch: pnpm install, oxfmt/oxlint touched files, core and core-test tsgo, focused Vitest 4 files / 62 tests, Codex review clean

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Sandbox doctor registry and image repairs now produce structured findings, changes, warnings, effects, dry-run output, and selected repair results while preserving the legacy sandbox contribution order.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, source checkout CLI, disposable HOME with isolated OpenClaw state dir.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a disposable legacy sandbox registry at `.openclaw/sandbox/containers.json`, then ran `openclaw doctor --lint --only core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files --json`, `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive --yes --no-workspace-suggestions`, and `openclaw doctor --lint --only core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files --json` again through the PR source checkout. Also ran `openclaw doctor --lint --only core/doctor/sandbox-scope --json` against a disposable config with shared sandbox scope and per-agent docker overrides.
Evidence after fix: Before fix, registry lint returned `ok:false` with one `core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files` warning: `Legacy sandbox containers registry file detected.` During fix, doctor printed `Migrated containers registry` and completed. After fix, registry lint returned `ok:true` with `findings:[]`, and the registry was present as a sharded file under `.openclaw/sandbox/containers/`. The sandbox-scope lint returned one `core/doctor/sandbox-scope` warning for ignored per-agent docker overrides under shared scope.
Observed result after fix: Legacy sandbox registry files are exposed as structured health findings, repaired at the existing sandbox contribution position, migrated to sharded registry storage, and then disappear from the same lint selector. Shared-scope sandbox override warnings are also exposed through the structured lint selector without repair mutation.
What was not tested: Full repo check in this rerun; live Docker image builds or mutation of a real user sandbox registry. The focused tests cover registry migration, image readiness/dry-run/effects, positional repair routing, broad repair exclusion, and non-fix note preservation.
target.author: giodl73-repo
